DIVERGE region code: first byte differs at offset 0x85 (guest 0x10085) -- 01 vs 81

Status: regressed (captured-window mismatch with the RPCS3 baseline).

Boot trajectory: CellGov now terminates at sys_process_exit with
code 0x80010005 (CELL_ESRCH-class) at PPU step 10,872 (PC
0x10381ce8). Before commit 8033644 (syscall-number alignment),
flOw reached step 1,402,388 and exited cleanly. PPU spec-fix
work (XER.SO propagation in compares, normalized unsigned CR0,
FP fused-multiply-add, sign-extension through store-buffer
forwarding, super-pair invalidation widening) subsequently shifted
flOw's exit-probe path by +904 steps (9,968 -> 10,872); SSHD and
WipEout were unaffected by the same fixes because their critical
paths do not exercise those corner cases.

The earlier exit means CellGov captures its observation at a much
earlier boot point than the RPCS3 baseline, so the cross-runner
code-region match no longer holds. The byte difference at offset
0x85 of the code region is a consequence of the captured-window
mismatch, not an independent bug. The RPCS3 baseline (rpcs3.dump)
was captured at the first sys_tty_write under headless mode and
is unchanged across CellGov revisions.

Verdict: pending investigation -- the early sys_process_exit needs
to be tracked back to the syscall whose corrected dispatch caused
the divergent control-flow. The current step-count floor is 10,872
(not 9,968).
